title: ugettxtc
text: ugettxtc Get additional data from a Uniface message or help text. Explain this code long ugettxtc (unsigned char* buffer, long max); Copy Parameters buffer (Output) The contents of the message or help text. max The maximum number of characters to be copied to the buffer pointed to by buffer . The size of the buffer should be at least max +1 bytes to accommodate the zero terminator character. Return Values Table 1. Values returned by ugettxt and ugettxtc Value Meaning >0 The number of bytes copied into the buffer 0 An error occurred <0 The negated number of bytes copied into the buffer. The negative status indicates that more data is available in the field, and that you should call ugettxtc to get additional data. Use Library: yrtl Description ugettxtc moves an additional chunk of data from a Uniface 4GL message or help text into the specified buffer . This function should only be called after a previous call to ugettxt or ugettxtc returned a negative value, which indicates there is more data available. Parameters and return values are the same as for ugettxt , except there is no parameter for the ID of the message or help text. So if ugettxtc also returns a negative status, it must be called repeatedly until a positive status is returned. See the "ugettxt" topic for additional information and example code. Example: ugettxt and ugettxtc The following example retrieves and prints the string value of the message or help text whose IdString is contained in general register $1 . To illustrate the use of repeated ugettxtc calls, we read in small chunks and grow the buffer as necessary, in the same way as in the ufget / ufgetc example. Explain this code EXPORT void TEST_UGETTXT () { #define CHUNKSIZE 12 char id[51]; long len; char* buf; long totlen; ugetregs(1, (unsigned char *)id, sizeof(id)-1); buf = malloc(CHUNKSIZE); len = ugettxt(id, buf, CHUNKSIZE); totlen = abs(len); while ( len < 0 ) { buf = realloc(buf, totlen+CHUNKSIZE); len = ugettxtc(buf+totlen, CHUNKSIZE); totlen += abs(len); } buf = realloc(buf, totlen+1); buf[totlen] = 0; u_printf("The content of id %s is : %s\n", id, buf); } Copy Related Links ugettxt Get the contents of a message or help text.
